headline: Inside the mutually beneficial relationship of shipping magnate Saadé and President Macron
description: 'The shipping giant at the heart of French power' (1/4) The French leader and the head of the company CMA CGM have developed a relationship that is as respectful as it is useful for both parties, given how closely the interests of the family-owned group and the French state are intertwined.

headline: Trump warns Middle East truce on 'massive life support,' as Iran says ready for any aggression
Hantavirus ship heads to the Netherlands after passengers flown home
Sandra Laugier, philosopher: 'In France as in the US, never has freedom of expression been so invoked by its enemies'
description: The recent crisis that erupted at Grasset is symptomatic of a new wave of brutality in the public sphere, argues academic Sandra Laugier for Le Monde. According to Laugier, popular culture is far more resistant than certain intellectual circles, which wrongly believe themselves to be protected from the far right's predations.

headline: Suspicious transactions on oil markets: 'On Wall Street, this sense of impunity is clearly widespread'
description: Insider trading is now commonplace on the New York Stock Exchange, where profits linked to oil sales connected to the situation in Iran are estimated to have reached several hundred million dollars, reports Le Monde's New York correspondent Nicolas Chapuis.

headline: Gaming clubs officially set up shop in Paris
description: In early 2026, seven gaming establishments, where slot machines are prohibited, were made permanent after an eight-year trial period. The massive Partouche Casino Club, which is not a traditional casino, will have its grand opening on Tuesday.

headline: In Los Angeles, the World Cup still struggles to find its place in the sun
description: One month before the kickoff of the 2026 World Cup, Le Monde correspondents share a glimpse of their country's relationship with football.

Paris's La Villette reinvents itself as an urban climate laboratory
description: Forty years after its inauguration by former French president François Mitterrand, the green lung of northeast Paris is transforming into a vibrant haven where animals, plants and children coexist.

headline: 'There are no innocents in Gaza': Channel 14, the Israeli television channel accused of genocidal rhetoric
description: The channel, whose ratings have been steadily increasing, gives prominence to the most violent rhetoric, including calls for the 'annihilation' of the Palestinian territory. Three Israeli NGOs have compiled a detailed record of these statements, to which Le Monde has had access, and have filed a petition before the Supreme Court.

headline: Women are featured on Cannes's poster, but still sidelined at the festival
description: The Cannes Film Festival's choice of a still from 'Thelma & Louise' for its official poster has drawn criticism from the feminist collective 50/50, which points to the persistently low number of female directors in the official selection.
